LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PALMERO ENTERPRISES, INC., SHAKIRA DALAL AND LALA MAMMEDOVA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, AS TO PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND JULIE MANGNEY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRAYER FOR RESTITUTION AND THE REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN. THERE IS NO CLAIM ALLEGED BY THE MANGNEYS THAT PROVIDES FOR RESTITUTION. NOR HAVE THE MANGNEYS ALLEGED THAT THEY ARE LIKELY TO SUFFER HARM IN THE FURTHER FROM THE DEFENDANTS AND THUS THERE IS NO BASIS FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THE LEGAL VALIDITY OF THE REQUEST IN THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "$500 PER DAY EACH DAY A VIOLATION WAS COMMITTED" IS, AS THE PARTIES ACKNOWLEDGE, CURRENTLY SUBJECT TO A SPLIT OF AUTHORITIES AND NEED NOT BE RESOLVED AT THIS POINT. ALL OF THE OTHER PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ARE SUFFICIENTLY SUPPORTED BY THE MANGNEYS' ALLEGATIONS OF ULTIMATE FACTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: VICKI GROSS, CSR 5525 (EMAIL: VGROSS@SFTC.ORG)

(206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TOVE NILSEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND JULIE MANGNEY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT MS. NILSEN HAD A CARETAKING RELATIONSHIP WITH MR. MANGNEY OR COMMITTED ANY ACTS OR OMISSIONS CONSTITUTING A VIOLATION OF THE ELDER ABUSE STATUTE OR NEGLIGENCE THAT WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING MR. MANGNEY'S CLAIMED INJURIES. THE ONLY SPECIFIC REFERENCE TO MS. NILSEN IN THE COMPLAINT IS THAT SHE OWNED, LEASED, OPERATED, ADMINISTERED, MANAGED, DIRECTED, AND/OR CONTROLLED AND IS A MANAGING AGENT OF THE ENTITY THAT DOES BUSINESS AS MOUNTAIN VIEW HEALTH CARE CENTER. THIS IS INSUFFICIENT TO STATE A CLAIM AGAINST MS. NILSEN FOR CONDUCT THAT AMOUNTS TO THE FAILURE OR NEGLECT TO PROVIDE NEEDED MEDICAL CARE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: VICKI GROSS, CSR 5525 (EMAIL: VGROSS@SFTC.ORG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TOVE NILSEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, AS TO PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND JULIE MANGNEY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON MS. NILSEN'S DEMURRER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: VICKI GROSS, CSR 5525 (EMAIL: VGROSS@SFTC.ORG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PALMERO ENTERPRISES, INC., SHAKIRA DALAL AND LALA MAMMEDOVA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND JULIE MANGNEY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION. WHILE NOT AS CLEAR AS IT COULD HAVE BEEN, THE COMPLAINT IS NOT UNCERTAIN AS TO WHO IS BEING SUED AND WHICH PLAINTIFFS ARE SUING FOR EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ALLEGED ON BEHALF OF BOTH PLAINTIFFS AND AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALLEGED BY MR. MANGNEY AGAINST PALMERO ONLY. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION ADEQUATELY ALLEGES A CLAIM FOR VIOLATION OF THE ELDER ABUSE STATUTE BY ALLEGING, AMONG OTHER THINGS, A CARETAKING AND CUSTODIAL RELATIONSHIP ASSUMED BY MOUNTAIN VIEW HEALTH CARE CENTER AND ITS RECKLESS AND OPPRESSIVE WITHHOLDING OF REQUIRED MEDICAL CARE PARTICIPATED IN AND RATIFIED BY ITS MANAGING AGENTS. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT RENDERED MOOT BY THE TENDER OF A $500 CHECK. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION ADEQUATELY ALLEGED VIOLATION OF THE PATIENT'S BILL OF RIGHTS AGAINST PALMERO. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: VICKI GROSS, CSR 5525 (EMAIL: VGROSS@SFTC.ORG)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PALMERO ENTERPRISES, INC., SHAKIRA DALAL AND LALA MAMMEDOVA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, AS TO PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND JULIE MANGNEY

Appearances: Jennifer E. Elgie Paez of Lewis Brisbois (415.438.6610) for defendants and moving parties; Joseph Nicholson of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger (415.981.7210) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED without leave to amend as to the prayer for restitution and the request for injunctive relief and denied as to all other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken. There is no claim alleged by the Mangneys that provides for restitution. Nor have the Mangneys alleged that they are likely to suffer harm in the further from the defendants and thus there is no basis for injunctive relief. The legal validity of the request in the second cause of action for "$500 per day each day a violation was committed" is, as the parties acknowledge, currently subject to a split of authorities and need not be resolved at this point. All of the other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken are sufficiently supported by the Mangneys' allegations of ultimate facts. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR 5525 (email: vgross@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PALMERO ENTERPRISES, INC., SHAKIRA DALAL AND LALA MAMMEDOVA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND JULIE MANGNEY

Appearances: Jennifer E. Elgie Paez of Lewis Brisbois (415.438.6610) for defendants and moving parties; Joseph Nicholson of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger (415.981.7210) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OVERRULED as to all three causes of action. While not as clear as it could have been, the complaint is not uncertain as to who is being sued and which plaintiffs are suing for each cause of action. The first and third causes of action are alleged on behalf of both plaintiffs and against all defendants. The second cause of action is alleged by Mr. Mangney against Palmero only. The first cause of action adequately alleges a claim for violation of the Elder Abuse Statute by alleging, among other things, a caretaking and custodial relationship assumed by Mountain View Health Care Center and its reckless and oppressive withholding of required medical care participated in and ratified by its managing agents. The second cause of action is not rendered moot by the tender of a $500 check. The second cause of action adequately alleged violation of the Patient's Bill of Rights against Palmero. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR 5525 (email: vgross@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TOVE NILSEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, AS TO PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND JULIE MANGNEY

Appearances: Jennifer E. Elgie Paez of Lewis Brisbois (415.438.6610) for defendant and moving party; Joseph Nicholson of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger (415.981.7210) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OFF CALENDAR in light of the ruling on Ms. Nilsen's demurrer. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR 5525 (email: vgross@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TOVE NILSEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS JOHN AND JULIE MANGNEY

Appearances: Jennifer E. Elgie Paez of Lewis Brisbois (415.438.6610) for defendant and moving party; Joseph Nicholson of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger (415.981.7210) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with five days leave to amend as to both causes of action. The complaint fails to allege ultimate facts showing that Ms. Nilsen had a caretaking relationship with Mr. Mangney or committed any acts or omissions constituting a violation of the Elder Abuse Statute or negligence that was a substantial factor in causing Mr. Mangney's claimed injuries. The only specific reference to Ms. Nilsen in the complaint is that she owned, leased, operated, administered, managed, directed, and/or controlled and is a managing agent of the entity that does business as Mountain View Health Care Center. This is insufficient to state a claim against Ms. Nilsen for conduct that amounts to the failure or neglect to provide needed medical care. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR 5525 (email: vgross@sftc.org) (302/HEK)
